title: Habenaria iyoensis
taxonomy: Habenaria iyoensis is a species of orchid in the genus Habenaria and the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the order Asparagales. Within the family Orchidaceae, Habenaria iyoensis is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. This orchid species is further classified into the tribe Orchideae. The classification of Habenaria iyoensis reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the same family and order.

intro: Habenaria iyoensis is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia, specifically in India and other parts of the Indian subcontinent. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria iyoensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Habenaria iyoensis is often prepared as a decoction or powder, or used in combination with other herbs to make herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, or teas.
